videos.title: Coronavirus Causing Value Of Bitcoin To Soar
videos.text: BUILDING INSPECTORS WILL HELP RESIDENTS DETERMINE IF THEIR HOMES ARE SAFE. OF THE CORONAVIRUS OUTBREAK, IT IS BOOSTING THE FORTUNE OF BITCOIN. THE VALUE HOVERING AT AROUND $9300 UP TO 10% OVER THE LAST WEEK. 30% SINCE THE END OF 2019 THE BEST START SINCE 2012.

videos.title: Expert Explains Bitcoin Surge Amid Coronavirus Outbreak
videos.text: there have been ongoing health concerns amid the corona virus outbreak but that's not the only field the virus is affecting Bitcoin is making a big comeback because of it joining me to talk about this is tim curry co-founder of the local blockchain BTM which operates a bitcoin Ian's in our area Tim thanks for joining us are we doing great so a little hard to sort of imagine what this is all about so Bitcoin is having one of the best starts of a year it's had in nearly a decade how is that connected to the corona virus well when any uncertainty and fears come into the market people tend to put their sell their assets in safe havens like gold and things like that but we're seeing more and more that people are participating in the Bitcoin economy and putting money into the Bitcoin and crypto currencies and that's I what I would describe more as a risk haven so one of the reasons being the corona virus outbreak which is based in China China being an economic giant this is having an impact on the markets how else is it doing that and how does that connect back to Bitcoin well both China and the US have been printing an incredible amount of money we've seen the stock market in China decreased by about 8 percent despite the government urging its people that everything is fine this you know the virus will pass everything will get back to normal don't take money out of the market and typically when governments have historically told their citizens not to take money out of the market that's so that they could be the first to take money out of the market and then the citizens end up losing money so bitcoin is an interesting asset because it's a non-correlated assets so in other words it doesn't behave like the stock markets and things like that and it doesn't necessarily have this it's not affected by by those things in the same ways that many correlated assets would be so putting a little bit of of one savings or assets into that into that class is something that balances of portfolio and gives it a hedge against some of the some of that risk so what you're saying is when traditional markets are affected by things like disease or political unrest Bitcoin tends to do well yeah it does and there's some other factors that are playing into that right now we have what's called a halving coming up and that is an increase in scarcity for the Bitcoin that'll will be going into circulation and Bitcoin halves every four years we have one coming up at the end of April beginning of May so the amount of Bitcoin that's coming into circulation will literally be cut in half so that increases the scarcity we're seeing that more participants are holding Bitcoin for longer so the supply that is available right now people are tending to hold and of the 21 million bitcoins that will ever be circulated about 85% of them are already in circulation so that's very interesting as well so that scarcity is coinciding with all these other factors including the coronavirus right and remember that bitcoin is a technology it's not a stagnant asset class it's something that's continuing to be built on one example of something that makes Bitcoin quite different is last month there was 50 1511 Bitcoin sent in a single transaction which was about a 450 million dollar transaction that was sent across the world or from wallet wallet for a fee of 23 cents that would be something that wouldn't be impossible to do with traditional money certainly with gold even with cash you'll be exorbitantly expensive so okay Bitcoin had a good January 2020 I know it's hard to predict the future of these markets but what is the near future at least look like for Bitcoin I mean it's gonna be an interesting ride there's gonna be a fear of missing out and then there's going to be another bubble certainly and so it's something that it would be irresponsible not to have a small portion of your portfolio into Bitcoin and maybe some top-performing crypto assets but it's certainly the most volatile of all asset classes that ever existed but again it's over the last 11 years it's been the best performing asset in six of those eleven years

videos.title: Will Coronavirus Shut Down Bitcoin?! Anthony "Pomp" Pompliano explains
videos.text: business about the impact of the coroner virus outbreak on Monday so the doubt wipe out year-to-date gains yet Bitcoin is around 30 percent so far this year far more over the last 12 months so is it a safe haven in stormy markets or something else entirely Morgan Creek digital assets co-founder and partner and flee popular node joins us now great to have you with us so thanks so much for having me here make two observations about their corner virus outbreak not that we want to take away from the tragedy that it represents but the first thing was mining because a lot of that happens in China yep thoughts their big thing is a lot of manufacturing plants are being shut down and so those companies are losing revenue but when it comes to mining computers are doing most of the work it takes very few humans so mining is not impacted and it's also very decentralized across the globe and so if you get kind of a virus outbreak in one geographic area this is necessary to affect the mining hash rate nearly as much and so I don't think that mining is really susceptible to the same things that like a traditional manufacturing company is okay we're going to come back to that I promise you but we'll move
